learningObjective: Understand vectors as objects with magnitude and direction that can be added and scaled, discovering how vector operations work geometrically through a basis recipe constructor
ahaMoment: Discovering that you can reach ANY target vector by adjusting just two coefficients — the basis vectors are fundamental building blocks, and the 'numbers' describing a vector are really instructions for combining basis vectors, not inherent properties of space itself

miniSpec.title: Basis Recipe Constructor
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: You're given two fixed 'basis' vectors (not perpendicular, not aligned) and a target vector shown in gray. You must construct the target using ONLY whole-number multiples of the two basis vectors—like a recipe using only cups (basis 1) and tablespoons (basis 2). A counter shows your ingredient budget; overspending causes overflow.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: Two integer spinner controls (up/down buttons or scroll wheels) that set the multiplier coefficients for each basis vector, changing how many copies of each basis vector are combined
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: As you increment each spinner, that many copies of the corresponding basis vector appear arranged tip-to-tail along the construction path; the resultant vector (sum) draws in bright color from origin to the final tip position; distance to target updates numerically and as a shrinking gap line
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Each basis vector contribution appears as a separate colored segment in the tip-to-tail chain (e.g., three blue arrows for '3× basis-1', two red arrows for '2× basis-2'); a component breakdown table shows how many units of each basis you're using; the resultant's coordinates appear in terms of the basis vectors, not x/y
miniSpec.trace_strategy: Each time you test a coefficient combination, the resultant vector freezes as a faint ghost with a label showing the coefficients tried (e.g., '2,3'); this creates a growing field of attempt vectors radiating from origin, visually mapping the integer lattice spanned by the two basis vectors
miniSpec.aha_moment: Discovering that you can reach ANY target vector by adjusting just two coefficients—the basis vectors are fundamental building blocks, and the 'numbers' describing a vector are really instructions for combining basis vectors, not inherent properties of space itself
miniSpec.feasibility_sketch: Draggable integer spinners, SVG vector drawing with tip-to-tail path construction, ghost trace layer, and a simple numeric readout table—all standard web primitives; no runtime risk beyond typical canvas repaint.
miniSpec.weakness: The 'overflow' budget mechanic is mentioned in narrative but never specified: unclear what overflow does, when it triggers, or whether it teaches or punishes—this vagueness could undermine the feedback informativeness claim if poorly implemented.

modelVariables.vbb_coeff1: Integer coefficient for basis vector 1 (teal), student-controlled via spinner
modelVariables.vbb_coeff2: Integer coefficient for basis vector 2 (indigo), student-controlled via spinner
modelVariables.vbb_resultX: derived: x-coordinate of the resultant vector (coeff1*basis1x + coeff2*basis2x)
modelVariables.vbb_resultY: derived: y-coordinate of the resultant vector (coeff1*basis1y + coeff2*basis2y)
modelVariables.vbb_distanceToTarget: derived: Euclidean distance from resultant to target vector
modelVariables.vbb_targetReached: derived: boolean, true when distance < threshold (target matched)
modelVariables.vbb_ghostTraces: array of past attempt coefficients [{c1, c2}], for ghost trace layer

assertions.description: On load, the coefficients are not set to the solution — the target is not yet reached
assertions.visualCheck: Target vector shown in gray, resultant does not coincide with it, gap line visible

assertions.description: Incrementing coeff1 adds another basis-1 segment to the tip-to-tail chain and moves the resultant
assertions.visualCheck: Two teal arrow segments appear tip-to-tail; resultant vector extends further from origin

assertions.description: Setting a negative coefficient makes that basis contribution point in the opposite direction
assertions.visualCheck: The teal segment now points opposite to its original direction

assertions.description: When coefficients match the target's decomposition, the target is reached (the aha moment)
assertions.visualCheck: Resultant overlaps target, gap line disappears or shrinks to near zero

assertions.description: After changing coefficients, a ghost trace appears recording the previous attempt
assertions.visualCheck: A faint ghost arrow from a previous attempt is visible with its coefficient label

invariants.description: The resultant vector always equals coeff1*basis1 + coeff2*basis2
Distance to target is always non-negative
